The comparison is inclusive (`>=`) and lives in the view layer; each         `minWeights[k]` is a caller argument (ADR-0042's `weight >= 0` is just the         conventional `minWeight = 0` a caller passes, not a baked-in rule). The kernel stays         weight-neutral. Empty arrays \u21d2 no exclusion (degenerates to the unfiltered page).         **Tag-target asymmetry (load-bearing, ADR-0054).** A descriptive-label TAG targets         different UIDs for folders vs files, and the predicate is a UNION over the viewed         lenses (mirroring the client's `FileBrowser.resolveTagSet`): an item is excluded iff         ANY viewed lens has an active `excludeTagDef` TAG (weight >= minWeight) on ANY DATA         UID resolved at the item across the viewed lenses (files), or on the item's anchor         UID (folders):           - **folder** item (anchorType == bytes32(0)): the TAG targets the item's ANCHOR             UID, bucket `ANCHOR_SCHEMA_UID`. Tested against every lens.           - **file** item: the TAG targets a DATA UID, reached via the placement PIN             `getActivePinTarget(itemAnchor, lens, dataSchemaUID)`, bucket `dataSchemaUID`.             The DATA UIDs of ALL viewed lenses are collected (deduplicated) ONCE per item,             then each is tested against EVERY lens's tags for EVERY exclude pair \u2014 so a DATA             one lens pinned and ANOTHER viewed lens tagged is still excluded. Testing an             exclude tag against a file's anchor UID is the wrong target and excludes             nothing \u2014 the known footgun.         `maxItems` counts POST-filter result slots: an excluded item advances the walker         but consumes no slot (same as a revoked / out-of-lens skip). A phase-1 scan budget         (`_FILE_SCAN_BUDGET_PER_CALL`) is added so a 100%-excluded page can't loop the whole         phase-1 source in one call \u2014 when the budget is hit before `maxItems` is filled, a         non-empty cursor is returned at the current position.         **Scan-budget scope.** The per-call budget bounds *this view's* phase-1 loop (the         number of candidates inspected here), NOT the work inside a single underlying         `indexer.getAnchorsBySchemaAndAddressList` call. // The recovery ID.\n    bytes32 r; // The x-coordinate of the nonce R.\n    bytes32 s; // The signature data.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing a single attestation.\nstruct Attestation {\n    bytes32 uid; // A unique identifier of the attestation.\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    uint64 time; // The time when the attestation was created (Unix timestamp).\n    uint64 expirationTime; // The time when the attestation expires (Unix timestamp).\n    uint64 revocationTime; // The time when the attestation was revoked (Unix timestamp).\n    bytes32 refUID; // The UID of the related attestation.\n    address recipient; // The recipient of the attestation.\n    address attester; // The attester/sender of the attestation.\n    bool revocable; // Whether the attestation is revocable.\n    bytes data; // Custom attestation data.\n}\n\n/// @notice A helper function to work with unchecked iterators in loops.\nfunction uncheckedInc(uint256 i) pure returns (uint256 j) {\n    unchecked {\n        j = i + 1;\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0x957bd2e6d0d6d637f86208b135c29fbaf4412cb08e5e7a61ede16b80561bf685","license":"MIT"},"@ethereum-attestation-service/eas-contracts/contracts/IEAS.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { ISchemaRegistry } from \"./ISchemaRegistry.sol\";\nimport { ISemver } from \"./ISemver.sol\";\nimport { Attestation, Signature } from \"./Common.sol\";\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the arguments of the attestation request.\nstruct AttestationRequestData {\n    address recipient; // The recipient of the attestation.\n    uint64 expirationTime; // The time when the attestation expires (Unix timestamp).\n    bool revocable; // Whether the attestation is revocable.\n    bytes32 refUID; // The UID of the related attestation.\n    bytes data; // Custom attestation data.\n    uint256 value; // An explicit ETH amount to send to the resolver. This is important to prevent accidental user errors.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the full arguments of the attestation request.\nstruct AttestationRequest {\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    AttestationRequestData data; // The arguments of the attestation request.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the full arguments of the full delegated attestation request.\nstruct DelegatedAttestationRequest {\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    AttestationRequestData data; // The arguments of the attestation request.\n    Signature signature; // The ECDSA signature data.\n    address attester; // The attesting account.\n    uint64 deadline; // The deadline of the signature/request.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the full arguments of the multi attestation request.\nstruct MultiAttestationRequest {\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    AttestationRequestData[] data; // The arguments of the attestation request.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the full arguments of the delegated multi attestation request.\nstruct MultiDelegatedAttestationRequest {\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    AttestationRequestData[] data; // The arguments of the attestation requests.\n    Signature[] signatures; // The ECDSA signatures data. Please note that the signatures are assumed to be signed with increasing nonces.\n    address attester; // The attesting account.\n    uint64 deadline; // The deadline of the signature/request.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the arguments of the revocation request.\nstruct RevocationRequestData {\n    bytes32 uid; // The UID of the attestation to revoke.\n    uint256 value; // An explicit ETH amount to send to the resolver. This is important to prevent accidental user errors.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the full arguments of the revocation request.\nstruct RevocationRequest {\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    RevocationRequestData data; // The arguments of the revocation request.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the arguments of the full delegated revocation request.\nstruct DelegatedRevocationRequest {\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    RevocationRequestData data; // The arguments of the revocation request.\n    Signature signature; // The ECDSA signature data.\n    address revoker; // The revoking account.\n    uint64 deadline; // The deadline of the signature/request.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the full arguments of the multi revocation request.\nstruct MultiRevocationRequest {\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    RevocationRequestData[] data; // The arguments of the revocation request.\n}\n\n/// @notice A struct representing the full arguments of the delegated multi revocation request.\nstruct MultiDelegatedRevocationRequest {\n    bytes32 schema; // The unique identifier of the schema.\n    RevocationRequestData[] data; // The arguments of the revocation requests.\n    Signature[] signatures; // The ECDSA signatures data. Please note that the signatures are assumed to be signed with increasing nonces.\n    address revoker; // The revoking account.\n    uint64 deadline; // The deadline of the signature/request.\n}\n\n/// @title IEAS\n/// @notice EAS - Ethereum Attestation Service interface.\ninterface IEAS is ISemver {\n    /// @notice Emitted when an attestation has been made.\n    /// @param recipient The recipient of the attestation.\n    /// @param attester The attesting account.\n    /// @param uid The UID of the new attestation.\n    /// @param schemaUID The UID of the schema.\n    event Attested(address indexed recipient, address indexed attester, bytes32 uid, bytes32 indexed schemaUID);\n\n    /// @notice Emitted when an attestation has been revoked.\n    /// @param recipient The recipient of the attestation.\n    /// @param attester The attesting account.\n    /// @param schemaUID The UID of the schema.\n    /// @param uid The UID the revoked attestation.\n    event Revoked(address indexed recipient, address indexed attester, bytes32 uid, bytes32 indexed schemaUID);\n\n    /// @notice Emitted when a data has been timestamped.\n    /// @param data The data.\n    /// @param timestamp The timestamp.\n    event Timestamped(bytes32 indexed data, uint64 indexed timestamp);\n\n    /// @notice Emitted when a data has been revoked.\n    /// @param revoker The address of the revoker.\n    /// @param data The data.\n    /// @param timestamp The timestamp.\n    event RevokedOffchain(address indexed revoker, bytes32 indexed data, uint64 indexed timestamp);\n\n    /// @notice Returns the address of the global schema registry.\n    /// @return The address of the global schema registry.\n    function getSchemaRegistry() external view returns (ISchemaRegistry);\n\n    /// @notice Attests to a specific schema.\n    /// @param request The arguments of the attestation request.\n    /// @return The UID of the new attestation.\n    ///\n    /// Example:\n    ///     attest({\n    ///         schema: \"0facc36681cbe2456019c1b0d1e7bedd6d1d40f6f324bf3dd3a4cef2999200a0\",\n    ///         data: {\n    ///             recipient: transfers.\n    function isPayable() external pure returns (bool);\n\n    /// @notice Processes an attestation and verifies whether it's valid.\n    /// @param attestation The new attestation.\n    /// @return Whether the attestation is valid.\n    function attest(Attestation calldata attestation) external payable returns (bool);\n\n    /// @notice Processes multiple attestations and verifies whether they are valid.\n    /// @param attestations The new attestations.\n    /// @param values Explicit ETH amounts which were sent with each attestation.\n    /// @return Whether all the an attestation revocation and verifies if it can be revoked.\n    /// @param attestation The existing attestation to be revoked.\n    /// @return Whether the attestation can be revoked.\n    function The existing attestations to be revoked.\n    /// @param values Explicit ETH amounts which were sent with each revocation.\n    /// @return Whether the attestations can be revoked.\n    function 0.8.26;\n\nimport { IEAS, Attestation } from \"@ethereum-attestation-service/eas-contracts/contracts/IEAS.sol\";\n\ninterface IEdgeResolverForFileView {\n    /// @notice TAG-specific lens-aware check: true iff any of `attesters` has an active\n    ///         TAG on (targetID, definition). Used for phase-0 folder visibility (ADR-0038):\n    ///         folder visibility is TAG-only; a PIN with `definition=DATA_SCHEMA_UID` targeting\n    ///         a folder must NOT make that folder appear in lens-scoped directory listings.\n    ///         One SLOAD per attester.\n    function hasActiveTagFromAny(\n        bytes32 targetID,\n        bytes32 definition,\n        address[] calldata attesters\n    ) external view returns (bool);\n\n    /// @notice Append-only discovery: child anchors with an active edge (PIN or TAG) under\n    ///         `definition`, scoped by `parentUID`. Used by Shape B folder-visibility reads\n    ///         (ADR-0038, ADR-0041).\n    function getChildrenWithEdge(\n        bytes32 parentUID,\n        bytes32 definition,\n        uint256 start,\n        uint256 length\n    ) external view returns (bytes32[] memory);\n    function getChildrenWithEdgeCount(bytes32 parentUID, bytes32 definition) external view returns (uint256);\n\n    /// @notice O(1) read of the active PIN's target UID for a (definition, attester, schema) slot.\n    ///         Primary read for Shape A consumers (file placement, PROPERTY value binding).\n    function getActivePinTarget(\n        bytes32 definition,\n        address attester,\n        bytes32 targetSchema\n    ) external view returns (bytes32);\n\n    /// @notice PIN-specific single-attester check: true iff `attester` has an active PIN on\n    ///         (targetID, definition). One SLOAD.\n    ///\n    ///         Use this \u2014 not `isActiveEdgeAnySchema` \u2014 for cross-attester file-placement dedup\n    ///         in `getFilesAtPath` (ADR-0041): file placement is PIN-only (Shape A); a TAG from\n    ///         an earlier attester must NOT suppress a later attester's valid PIN placement.\n    function isActivePinEdge(address attester, bytes32 targetID, bytes32 definition) external view returns (bool);\n\n    /// @notice O(1) read of the raw stored weight of the active TAG `(definition, attester,\n    ///         targetSchema)` whose target is `target`, or `(false, 0)` if none. Kernel\n    ///         weight-neutral \u2014 returns the raw weight; the caller applies any threshold\n    ///         policy (ADR-0054). Used by `getDirectoryPageFiltered` for tag-exclusion.\n    function getActiveTagWeight(\n        address attester,\n        bytes32 target,\n        bytes32 definition,\n        bytes32 targetSchema\n    ) external view returns (bool exists, int256 weight);\n}\n\ninterface IEFSIndexer {\n    function getChildren(\n        bytes32 anchorUID,\n        uint256 start,\n        uint256 length,\n        bool reverseOrder,\n        bool showRevoked\n    ) external view returns (bytes32[] memory);\n    function getChildAt(bytes32 parentAnchor, uint256 idx) external view returns (bytes32);\n    function getChildrenByAddressList(\n        bytes32 parentUID,\n        address[] calldata attesters,\n        uint256 startCursor,\n        uint256 pageSize,\n        bool reverseOrder,\n        bool showRevoked\n    ) external view returns (bytes32[] memory results, uint256 nextCursor);\n    function getAnchorsBySchemaAndAddressList(\n        bytes32 parentUID,\n        bytes32 anchorSchema,\n        address[] calldata attesters,\n        uint256 startCursor,\n        uint256 pageSize,\n        bool reverseOrder,\n        bool showRevoked\n    ) external view returns (bytes32[] memory results, uint256 nextCursor);\n    function getChildrenCount(bytes32 anchorUID) external view returns (uint256);\n    function getChildCountBySchema(bytes32 parentAnchor, bytes32 schema) external view returns (uint256);\n    function getReferencingAttestationCount(bytes32 targetUID, bytes32 schemaUID) external view returns (uint256);\n    function containsAttestations(bytes32 targetUID, address attester) external view returns (bool);\n    function isRevoked(bytes32 uid) external view returns (bool);\n    function getEAS() external view returns (IEAS);\n    function DATA_SCHEMA_UID() external view returns (bytes32);\n    function PROPERTY_SCHEMA_UID() external view returns (bytes32);\n    function MIRROR_SCHEMA_UID() external view returns (bytes32);\n    function ANCHOR_SCHEMA_UID() external view returns (bytes32);\n    function getReferencingAttestations(\n        bytes32 targetUID,\n        bytes32 schemaUID,\n        uint256 start,\n        uint256 length,\n        bool reverseOrder,\n        bool showRevoked\n    ) external view returns (bytes32[] memory);\n    function getReferencingBySchemaAndAttester(\n        bytes32 targetUID,\n        bytes32 schemaUID,\n        address attester,\n        uint256 start,\n        uint256 length,\n        bool reverseOrder,\n        bool showRevoked\n    ) external view returns (bytes32[] memory);\n}\n\ncontract EFSFileView {\n    struct FileSystemItem {\n        bytes32 uid;\n        string name;\n        bytes32 parentUID;\n        bool isFolder;\n        bool hasData;\n        uint256 childCount;\n        uint256 propertyCount;\n        uint64 timestamp;\n        address attester;\n        bytes32 schema; // Anchor Schema Type\n        bytes32 contentHash; // For DATA attestations\n    }\n\n    struct MirrorItem {\n        bytes32 uid;\n        bytes32 transportDefinition;\n        string uri;\n        address attester;\n        uint64 timestamp;\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Parallel-array tag-exclusion policy for `getDirectoryPageFiltered` (ADR-0054).\n    /// @dev    Bundles the `(excludeTagDefs[k], minWeights[k])` pairs into one memory value so the\n    ///         exclusion policy travels as a single stack slot through the phase walkers and into\n    ///         `_isItemExcluded` (keeps `viaIR` under the EVM stack limit). `defs.length` must equal\n    ///         `minWeights.length`; an empty policy means \"exclude nothing\".\n    struct ExcludeFilter {\n        bytes32[] defs;\n        int256[] minWeights;\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Opaque-cursor page result for multi-source views. See ADR-0036.\n    /// @dev    Callers treat `nextCursor` as opaque bytes: pass it back verbatim for the next\n    ///         page, or treat `nextCursor.length == 0` as \"fully walked, no more pages.\"\n    ///         The internal encoding is documented in ADR-0036 but may change across\n    ///         `EFSFileView` deploys; clients must not introspect it. Round-trips only within\n    ///         a single deploy are guaranteed.\n    struct DirectoryPage {\n        FileSystemItem[] items;\n        bytes nextCursor;\n    }\n\n    IEFSIndexer public immutable indexer;\n    IEAS public immutable eas;\n    IEdgeResolverForFileView public immutable edgeResolver;\n\n    constructor(IEFSIndexer _indexer, IEdgeResolverForFileView _edgeResolver) {\n        indexer = _indexer;\n        eas = _indexer.getEAS();\n        edgeResolver = _edgeResolver;\n    }\n\n    function getDirectoryPage(\n        bytes32 parentAnchor,\n        uint256 start,\n        uint256 length,\n        bytes32 dataSchemaUID,\n        bytes32 propertySchemaUID\n    ) external view returns (FileSystemItem[] memory) {\n        bytes32[] memory uids = indexer.getChildren(parentAnchor, start, length, true, false);\n        return _buildFileSystemItems(uids, parentAnchor, dataSchemaUID, propertySchemaUID);\n    }\n\n    function getDirectoryPageByAddressList(\n        bytes32 parentAnchor,\n        address[] memory attesters,\n        uint256 startingCursor,\n        uint256 pageSize\n    ) external view returns (FileSystemItem[] memory items, uint256 nextCursor) {\n        (bytes32[] memory resolvedUIDs, uint256 nextCur) = indexer.getChildrenByAddressList(\n            parentAnchor,\n            attesters,\n            startingCursor,\n            pageSize,\n            true,\n            false\n        );\n\n        items = _buildFileSystemItems(\n            resolvedUIDs,\n            parentAnchor,\n            indexer.DATA_SCHEMA_UID(),\n            indexer.PROPERTY_SCHEMA_UID()\n        );\n\n        return (items, nextCur);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Maximum attesters per multi-lens view call.\n    ///      Bounds per-call gas on the attester-scoped walkers; does not bound returned items.\n    ///      Callers wanting more than this many lenses need a different aggregation model.\n    uint256 private constant MAX_ATTESTERS_PER_QUERY = 20;\n\n    /// @dev Maximum exclude-tag predicates per `getDirectoryPageFiltered` call (ADR-0054).\n    ///      The per-item exclusion check loops over `excludeTagDefs`, so the per-item file branch\n    ///      is O(dataUIDs \u00d7 lenses \u00d7 excludeTags); this cap keeps that product bounded alongside\n    ///      `MAX_ATTESTERS_PER_QUERY`. 8 comfortably covers the explorer's `system` + `nsfw` policy\n    ///      (and headroom for future label policies) without letting a caller blow the per-item gas\n    ///      budget. Symmetric in spirit to `MAX_ATTESTERS_PER_QUERY`.\n    uint256 private constant MAX_EXCLUDE_TAGS_PER_QUERY = 8;\n\n    /// @dev Internal batch size for `_childrenWithEdge` chunk fetches during the folder\n    ///      phase of `getDirectoryPageBySchemaAndAddressList`. Chosen to keep memory use\n    ///      bounded while still amortizing external-call overhead.\n    uint256 private constant _FOLDER_SCAN_CHUNK = 64;\n\n    /// @dev Hard cap on entries inspected in phase 0 per call. The append-only\n    ///      `_childrenWithEdge[parent][schema]` list can grow unboundedly for a hot\n    ///      directory, and a page where most entries get filtered out (wrong attester,\n    ///      revoked) would otherwise loop `_childrenWithEdge.length` times in a\n    ///      single eth_call \u2014 causing RPC timeouts or provider out-of-gas. The budget\n    ///      bounds per-call work; the opaque cursor continues progress across calls\n    ///      (same pattern as ADR-0020's `MAX_PAGES = 10` mirror-scan cap in\n    ///      `EFSRouter._getBestMirrorURI`).\n    ///\n    ///      Read through `_folderScanBudgetPerCall()` (not the bare constant) so a\n    ///      test-only subclass can override it to a small value and exercise the budget\n    ///      guard without seeding thousands of items. Production default is unchanged.\n    uint256 private constant _FOLDER_SCAN_BUDGET_PER_CALL = 2048;\n\n    /// @dev Hard cap on phase-1 entries inspected per call in `getDirectoryPageFiltered`.\n    ///      Symmetric to `_FOLDER_SCAN_BUDGET_PER_CALL` (phase 0). The plain\n    ///      `getDirectoryPageBySchemaAndAddressList` does not need a phase-1 budget because every\n    ///      phase-1 candidate the indexer returns becomes a result item (no per-item drop), so its\n    ///      inner loop is naturally bounded by `maxItems`. i < uids.length; i++) {\n            bytes32 uid = uids[i];\n            Attestation memory att = eas.getAttestation(uid);\n\n            string memory name = \"\";\n            bytes32 anchorType = bytes32(0);\n            if (att.data.length > 0) {\n                (name, anchorType) = abi.decode(att.data, (string, bytes32));\n            }\n\n            uint256 childCount = indexer.getChildrenCount(uid);\n            uint256 dataCount = indexer.getReferencingAttestationCount(uid, dataSchemaUID);\n            uint256 propertyCount = indexer.getReferencingAttestationCount(uid, propertySchemaUID);\n\n            result[i] = FileSystemItem({\n                uid: uid,\n                name: name,\n                parentUID: parentAnchor,\n                isFolder: anchorType == bytes32(0), // generic anchor = folder regardless of children\n                hasData: dataCount > 0,\n                childCount: childCount,\n                propertyCount: propertyCount,\n                timestamp: att.time,\n                attester: att.attester,\n                schema: anchorType,\n                contentHash: bytes32(0)\n            });\n        }\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice PIN-based file listing with cross-attester dedup. Opaque-cursor paginated\n     *         (ADR-0036, ADR-0041). For each attester, reads the active PIN target\n     *         (cardinality 1) at `_activeBySlot[anchorUID][attester][schema]` \u2014 Shape A\n     *         file-placement reads.\n     *\n     *         Earlier attesters win on duplicate targets (ADR-0031 first-attester-wins).\n     *         Filtered-out entries (targets claimed by an earlier attester) still advance\n     *         the walker; `maxItems` bounds result size, not work.\n     *\n     * @param anchorUID  The path anchor (e.g. /memes/).\n     * @param attesters  Lens addresses to query, in precedence order.\n     * @param schema     Target schema to filter. **Must be DATA_SCHEMA_UID or\n     *                   ANCHOR_SCHEMA_UID** \u2014 those are the only two payload shapes\n     *                   the per-item decode below understands. Calling with any other\n     *                   schema (e.g. PROPERTY_SCHEMA_UID) will produce decode reverts\n     *                   or garbage `name`/`contentHash` fields. A separate, schema-aware\n     *                   listing view is the right home for non-file PIN-shaped reads.\n     * @param cursor     Opaque token from a prior call. Empty = start from the beginning.\n     * @param maxItems   Target result size. Must be > 0.\n     * @return page      `items` + `nextCursor`. `nextCursor.length == 0` iff every attester\n     *                   has been walked.\n     *\n     * @dev Note: with PIN cardinality 1 per attester, the per-attester result is at most\n     *      one item, so concurrent-mutation caveats from the prior TAG-list pagination no\n     *      longer apply.\n     */\n    function getFilesAtPath(\n        bytes32 anchorUID,\n        address[] calldata attesters,\n        bytes32 schema,\n        bytes memory cursor,\n        uint256 maxItems\n    ) external view returns (DirectoryPage memory page) {\n        require(attesters.length > 0, \"Attesters list cannot be empty\");\n        require(attesters.length <= MAX_ATTESTERS_PER_QUERY, \"Too many attesters\");\n        require(maxItems > 0, \"maxItems must be > 0\");\n\n        // Decode cursor \u2014 empty OR malformed = fresh start at attesterIdx=0.\n        // Same defensive pattern as `getDirectoryPageBySchemaAndAddressList`: length-check\n        // protects against `abi.decode` Panics on arbitrary caller-supplied bytes.\n        uint256 attesterIdx = 0;\n        if (cursor.length == 32) {\n            attesterIdx = abi.decode(cursor, (uint256));\n        }\n\n        bytes32[] memory buf = new bytes32[](maxItems);\n        // Winning placement (lens) attester for each buffered target, kept in lockstep with\n        // `buf`. The item's `attester` must be the attester whose active PIN won the walk \u2014\n        // NOT the DATA attestation author. With hardlinks/dedup the two differ (Alice can\n        // place Bob's DATA in her lens), and clients scope follow-up PROPERTY/MIRROR reads\n        // to the winning lens (ADR-0013, ADR-0014). This matches EFSRouter._findDataAtPath,\n        // which returns the placement attester, not the DATA author.\n        address[] memory bufAttesters = new address[](maxItems);\n        uint256 count = 0;\n\n        while (attesterIdx < attesters.length && count < maxItems) {\n            address currentAttester = attesters[attesterIdx];\n            attesterIdx++;\n\n            // O(1) PIN read \u2014 per-attester slot holds 0 or 1 target.\n            bytes32 target = edgeResolver.getActivePinTarget(anchorUID, currentAttester, schema);\n            if (target == bytes32(0)) continue;\n\n            // Cross-attester dedup (ADR-0031 first-attester-wins): if an earlier attester\n            // already has an active PIN placing this DATA at this anchor, skip \u2014 this lens\n            // is already represented. PIN-specific check: file placement is Shape A (PIN only).\n            // A TAG from an earlier attester must NOT suppress a later attester's valid PIN.\n            bool taken = false;\n            for (uint256 prior = 0; prior + 1 < attesterIdx; prior++) {\n                if (edgeResolver.isActivePinEdge(attesters[prior], target, anchorUID)) {\n                    taken = true;\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n            if (taken) continue;\n\n            bufAttesters[count] = currentAttester;\n            buf[count++] = target;\n        }\n\n        // Trim buffers, build items. `buf` and `bufAttesters` advanced in lockstep, so the\n        // same `count` trims both.\n        assembly (\"memory-safe\") {\n            mstore(buf, count)\n            mstore(bufAttesters, count)\n        }\n\n        bytes32 dataSchemaUID = indexer.DATA_SCHEMA_UID();\n        FileSystemItem[] memory items = new FileSystemItem[](count);\n        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n            Attestation memory att = eas.getAttestation(buf[i]);\n\n            string memory name = \"\";\n\n            if (att.schema != dataSchemaUID) {\n                // Anchor: decode name. DATA carries no inline fields (ADR-0049), so there is\n                // nothing to decode in the DATA branch. Guard on non-empty data \u2014 matching the two other\n                // anchor-decode sites in this contract \u2014 because EAS permits a zero-length `data` field\n                // on any schema, and an unguarded abi.decode of empty bytes panics and would brick the\n                // whole listing page for one malformed item.\n                bytes32 anchorType;\n                if (att.data.length > 0) {\n                    (name, anchorType) = abi.decode(att.data, (string, bytes32));\n                }\n            }\n\n            items[i] = FileSystemItem({\n                uid: buf[i],\n                name: name,\n                parentUID: anchorUID,\n                isFolder: att.schema != dataSchemaUID,\n                hasData: att.schema == dataSchemaUID,\n                childCount: 0,\n                propertyCount: 0,\n                timestamp: att.time,\n                // Placement (lens) attester whose active PIN won the walk \u2014 NOT\n                // `att.attester` (the DATA author). See `bufAttesters` declaration above.\n                attester: bufAttesters[i],\n                schema: att.schema,\n                // AGENT-NOTE: hash/size now live as PROPERTYs (ADR-0049), not DATA fields.\n                // Surfacing them in listings is future on-chain property-index work.\n                contentHash: bytes32(0)\n            });\n        }\n        page.items = items;\n\n        // Cursor: empty iff every attester walked.\n        if (attesterIdx >= attesters.length) {\n            page.nextCursor = \"\";\n        } else {\n            page.nextCursor = abi.encode(attesterIdx);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Get a lens's mirrors (retrieval methods) for a DATA attestation. **Lens-scoped** (ADR-0013).\n     * @dev The default mirror read: returns only `attester`'s active mirrors on `dataUID`, matching the\n     *      router's lens-scoped mirror selection \u2014 so a foreign attester's mirror (arbitrary-scheme bytes\n     *      since ADR-0056) can never surface to a viewer who didn't opt into that attester. Reads ARE\n     *      lens-scoped (overview.md load-bearing invariants), so the lens `attester` is a REQUIRED\n     *      parameter. For the rare cross-attester discovery case (debug/indexing) use\n     *      `getDataMirrorsAllAttesters`, which is explicitly NOT lens-scoped.\n     * @param dataUID   The DATA attestation UID\n     * @param attester  The lens attester to scope to (required)\n     * @param start     Pagination offset\n     * @param length    Page size\n     */\n    function getDataMirrors(\n        bytes32 dataUID,\n        address attester,\n        uint256 start,\n        uint256 length\n    ) external view returns (MirrorItem[] memory) {\n        bytes32 mirrorSchemaUID = indexer.MIRROR_SCHEMA_UID();\n        bytes32[] memory mirrorUIDs = indexer.getReferencingBySchemaAndAttester(\n            dataUID,\n            mirrorSchemaUID,\n            attester,\n            start,\n            length,\n            false, // reverseOrder\n            false // showRevoked \u2014 serves active mirrors (re-checked below)\n        );\n        return _collectActiveMirrors(mirrorUIDs);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Get mirrors for a DATA across **ALL** attesters \u2014 NOT lens-scoped. Debug / discovery only.\n     * @dev Returns every attester's mirrors, including ones from attesters the viewer never trusted.\n     *      Mirror URIs are attester-controlled arbitrary bytes (any scheme, ADR-0056). A consumer of this\n     *      MUST NOT render a foreign URI as a live link or auto-fetch it \u2014 each `MirrorItem.attester` is\n     *      returned so it can be labelled/filtered. Production reads should use the lens-scoped\n     *      `getDataMirrors(dataUID, attester, \u2026)`; this exists only for cross-attester inspection.\n     * @param dataUID  The DATA attestation UID\n     * @param start    Pagination offset\n     * @param length   Page size\n     */\n    function getDataMirrorsAllAttesters(\n        bytes32 dataUID,\n        uint256 start,\n        uint256 length\n    ) external view returns (MirrorItem[] memory) {\n        bytes32 mirrorSchemaUID = indexer.MIRROR_SCHEMA_UID();\n        bytes32[] memory mirrorUIDs = indexer.getReferencingAttestations(\n            dataUID,\n            mirrorSchemaUID,\n            start,\n            length,\n            false, // reverseOrder\n            false // showRevoked \u2014 serves active mirrors (re-checked below)\n        );\n        return _collectActiveMirrors(mirrorUIDs);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Shared tail of the mirror reads: drop revoked, decode (transportDefinition, uri), build items.\n    function _collectActiveMirrors(bytes32[] memory mirrorUIDs) private view returns (MirrorItem[] memory) {\n        uint256 activeCount = 0;\n        for (uint256 i = 0; i < mirrorUIDs.length; i++) {\n            if (!indexer.isRevoked(mirrorUIDs[i])) activeCount++;\n        }\n        MirrorItem[] memory result = new MirrorItem[](activeCount);\n        uint256 idx = 0;\n        for (uint256 i = 0; i < mirrorUIDs.length; i++) {\n            if (indexer.isRevoked(mirrorUIDs[i])) continue;\n            Attestation memory att = eas.getAttestation(mirrorUIDs[i]);\n            (bytes32 transportDef, string memory uri) = abi.decode(att.data, (bytes32, string));\n            result[idx++] = MirrorItem({\n                uid: mirrorUIDs[i],\n                transportDefinition: transportDef,\n                uri: uri,\n                attester: att.attester,\n                timestamp: att.time\n            });\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Deprecated content-hash \u2192 canonical DATA reverse lookup. Always returns bytes32(0).\n     * @dev AGENT-NOTE: hash/size now live as PROPERTYs (ADR-0049); reverse-lookup is future\n     *      on-chain property-index work. DATA is empty/pure-identity, so there is no longer an\n     *      intrinsic content-hash index; `dataByContentKey` is no longer written. Canonical/dedup\n     *      resolution moves to the REDIRECT primitive (ADR-0050) + the property index. The method\n     *      is retained as a no-op so the view ABI stays stable for callers during the transition.\n     */\n    function getCanonicalData(bytes32 /* contentHash */) external pure returns (bytes32) {\n        return bytes32(0);\n    }\n\n    function decodeName(bytes memory data) external pure returns (string memory) {\n        (string memory name, ) = abi.decode(data, (string, bytes32));\n        return name;\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0x7aab0baa6b0d3dbb970485d76e585c30f2b3cab9dcf39c8b623e800df3adda4c","license":"MIT"}},"version":1}

getCanonicalData(bytes32).details: AGENT-NOTE: hash/size now live as PROPERTYs (ADR-0049); reverse-lookup is future      on-chain property-index work. DATA is empty/pure-identity, so there is no longer an      intrinsic content-hash index; `dataByContentKey` is no longer written. Canonical/dedup      resolution moves to the REDIRECT primitive (ADR-0050) + the property index. The method      is retained as a no-op so the view ABI stays stable for callers during the transition.

getDataMirrors(bytes32,address,uint256,u.details: The default mirror read: returns only `attester`'s active mirrors on `dataUID`, matching the      router's lens-scoped mirror selection — so a foreign attester's mirror (arbitrary-scheme bytes      since ADR-0056) can never surface to a viewer who didn't opt into that attester. Reads ARE      lens-scoped (overview.md load-bearing invariants), so the lens `attester` is a REQUIRED      parameter. For the rare cross-attester discovery case (debug/indexing) use      `getDataMirrorsAllAttesters`, which is explicitly NOT lens-scoped.

params.attester: The lens attester to scope to (required)

getDataMirrorsAllAttesters(bytes32,uint2.details: Returns every attester's mirrors, including ones from attesters the viewer never trusted.      Mirror URIs are attester-controlled arbitrary bytes (any scheme, ADR-0056). A consumer of this      MUST NOT render a foreign URI as a live link or auto-fetch it — each `MirrorItem.attester` is      returned so it can be labelled/filtered. Production reads should use the lens-scoped      `getDataMirrors(dataUID, attester, …)`; this exists only for cross-attester inspection.

params.anchorSchema: Schema to filter on. Generic folders with an active tag of this                      schema are included in phase 0; direct children of this schema                      are included in phase 1.
params.attesters: Lens addresses (ADR-0031). An entry qualifies if ANY listed                      attester has contributed it.
params.cursor: Opaque token from a prior call. Empty bytes = start from the                      beginning. Never introspected by callers; encoding is an                      implementation detail (see ADR-0036).

returns.page: `items` + `nextCursor`. `nextCursor.length == 0` iff both phases                      are fully walked; otherwise pass `nextCursor` back verbatim.

params.anchorSchema: Schema to filter on (same role as the sibling function).
params.cursor: Opaque token from a prior call (ADR-0036); empty = fresh start.
params.excludeTagDefs: TAG predicates whose presence (with `weight >= minWeights[k]`) excludes.                      Parallel to `minWeights`. Empty ⇒ no exclusion (unfiltered page).
params.maxItems: Target POST-filter result size. Must be > 0.
params.minWeights: Inclusive weight thresholds, one per `excludeTagDefs[k]`. Caller-chosen                      policy; `minWeights.length` must equal `excludeTagDefs.length`.

returns.page: `items` (excluded items omitted) + `nextCursor`. `nextCursor.length == 0`                      iff both phases are fully walked.

getFilesAtPath(bytes32,address[],bytes32.details: Note: with PIN cardinality 1 per attester, the per-attester result is at most      one item, so concurrent-mutation caveats from the prior TAG-list pagination no      longer apply.

params.attesters: Lens addresses to query, in precedence order.
params.cursor: Opaque token from a prior call. Empty = start from the beginning.
params.schema: Target schema to filter. **Must be DATA_SCHEMA_UID or                   ANCHOR_SCHEMA_UID** — those are the only two payload shapes                   the per-item decode below understands. Calling with any other                   schema (e.g. PROPERTY_SCHEMA_UID) will produce decode reverts                   or garbage `name`/`contentHash` fields. A separate, schema-aware                   listing view is the right home for non-file PIN-shaped reads.

returns.page: `items` + `nextCursor`. `nextCursor.length == 0` iff every attester                   has been walked.

MAX_ATTESTERS_PER_QUERY.details: Maximum attesters per multi-lens view call.      Bounds per-call gas on the attester-scoped walkers; does not bound returned items.      Callers wanting more than this many lenses need a different aggregation model.

MAX_EXCLUDE_TAGS_PER_QUERY.details: Maximum exclude-tag predicates per `getDirectoryPageFiltered` call (ADR-0054).      The per-item exclusion check loops over `excludeTagDefs`, so the per-item file branch      is O(dataUIDs × lenses × excludeTags); this cap keeps that product bounded alongside      `MAX_ATTESTERS_PER_QUERY`. 8 comfortably covers the explorer's `system` + `nsfw` policy      (and headroom for future label policies) without letting a caller blow the per-item gas      budget. Symmetric in spirit to `MAX_ATTESTERS_PER_QUERY`.

_FILE_SCAN_BUDGET_PER_CALL.details: Hard cap on phase-1 entries inspected per call in `getDirectoryPageFiltered`.      Symmetric to `_FOLDER_SCAN_BUDGET_PER_CALL` (phase 0). The plain      `getDirectoryPageBySchemaAndAddressList` does not need a phase-1 budget because every      phase-1 candidate the indexer returns becomes a result item (no per-item drop), so its      inner loop is naturally bounded by `maxItems`. The filtered variant can DROP phase-1      items (the exclusion predicate), so a page that is 100%-excluded under the lens would      otherwise loop the entire phase-1 source in one eth_call. This budget bounds per-call      work; the opaque cursor (ADR-0036) continues progress across calls — same pattern as      the phase-0 budget and ADR-0020's mirror-scan cap.      Read through `_fileScanBudgetPerCall()` (not the bare constant) so a test-only      subclass can override it to a small value and exercise the budget guard without      seeding thousands of items. Production default is unchanged.

_FOLDER_SCAN_BUDGET_PER_CALL.details: Hard cap on entries inspected in phase 0 per call. The append-only      `_childrenWithEdge[parent][schema]` list can grow unboundedly for a hot      directory, and a page where most entries get filtered out (wrong attester,      revoked) would otherwise loop `_childrenWithEdge.length` times in a      single eth_call — causing RPC timeouts or provider out-of-gas. The budget      bounds per-call work; the opaque cursor continues progress across calls      (same pattern as ADR-0020's `MAX_PAGES = 10` mirror-scan cap in      `EFSRouter._getBestMirrorURI`).      Read through `_folderScanBudgetPerCall()` (not the bare constant) so a      test-only subclass can override it to a small value and exercise the budget      guard without seeding thousands of items. Production default is unchanged.

_FOLDER_SCAN_CHUNK.details: Internal batch size for `_childrenWithEdge` chunk fetches during the folder      phase of `getDirectoryPageBySchemaAndAddressList`. Chosen to keep memory use      bounded while still amortizing external-call overhead.

getCanonicalData(bytes32).notice: Deprecated content-hash → canonical DATA reverse lookup. Always returns bytes32(0).

getDataMirrors(bytes32,address,uint256,u.notice: Get a lens's mirrors (retrieval methods) for a DATA attestation. **Lens-scoped** (ADR-0013).

getDataMirrorsAllAttesters(bytes32,uint2.notice: Get mirrors for a DATA across **ALL** attesters — NOT lens-scoped. Debug / discovery only.

getDirectoryPageBySchemaAndAddressList(b.notice: Schema-aware directory listing with folder inclusion. Opaque-cursor paginated         (ADR-0036). Walks two underlying sources in order, dedup-free because the         sources are disjoint (tagged folders vs. direct children of `anchorSchema`):           Phase 0: qualifying generic folders — subfolders under `parentAnchor` that                    at least one `attesters[]` entry has an active TAG on with                    `definition = anchorSchema` (ADR-0006 revised, ADR-0038, ADR-0041:                    single-source tag-only folder visibility under the cardinality-N                    TAG schema).           Phase 1: direct child anchors of `anchorSchema` under `parentAnchor`, scoped                    to `attesters[]` via `_childrenBySchema` + `containsAttestations`.         Each call advances internal walkers by whatever it takes to produce up to         `maxItems` items or exhaust both sources. Filtered-out entries (revoked,         lens-out-of-scope) still advance the walker — `maxItems` bounds result         size, not work.

getDirectoryPageFiltered(bytes32,bytes32.notice: Tag-exclusion-filtered directory listing (ADR-0054). Identical walk, sources,         budgets, and opaque cursor format (ADR-0036) to         `getDirectoryPageBySchemaAndAddressList`, PLUS a per-item exclusion predicate over         a set of `(excludeTagDefs[k], minWeights[k])` pairs (parallel arrays, ADR-0054):           **Skip an item if, for ANY pair k, ANY attester in `attesters` has an active TAG           `excludeTagDefs[k]` on it with `weight >= minWeights[k]`.**         The exclusion is a UNION across the exclude-tag pairs AND across the viewed lenses —         each pair applies the exact single-tag semantic the v1 form did (the multi-tag form         supersedes the single-tag v1 so the explorer can hide e.g. `system` + `nsfw` in one         call). The comparison is inclusive (`>=`) and lives in the view layer; each         `minWeights[k]` is a caller argument (ADR-0042's `weight >= 0` is just the         conventional `minWeight = 0` a caller passes, not a baked-in rule). The kernel stays         weight-neutral. Empty arrays ⇒ no exclusion (degenerates to the unfiltered page).         **Tag-target asymmetry (load-bearing, ADR-0054).** A descriptive-label TAG targets         different UIDs for folders vs files, and the predicate is a UNION over the viewed         lenses (mirroring the client's `FileBrowser.resolveTagSet`): an item is excluded iff         ANY viewed lens has an active `excludeTagDef` TAG (weight >= minWeight) on ANY DATA         UID resolved at the item across the viewed lenses (files), or on the item's anchor         UID (folders):           - **folder** item (anchorType == bytes32(0)): the TAG targets the item's ANCHOR             UID, bucket `ANCHOR_SCHEMA_UID`. Tested against every lens.           - **file** item: the TAG targets a DATA UID, reached via the placement PIN             `getActivePinTarget(itemAnchor, lens, dataSchemaUID)`, bucket `dataSchemaUID`.             The DATA UIDs of ALL viewed lenses are collected (deduplicated) ONCE per item,             then each is tested against EVERY lens's tags for EVERY exclude pair — so a DATA             one lens pinned and ANOTHER viewed lens tagged is still excluded. Testing an             exclude tag against a file's anchor UID is the wrong target and excludes             nothing — the known footgun.         `maxItems` counts POST-filter result slots: an excluded item advances the walker         but consumes no slot (same as a revoked / out-of-lens skip). A phase-1 scan budget         (`_FILE_SCAN_BUDGET_PER_CALL`) is added so a 100%-excluded page can't loop the whole         phase-1 source in one call — when the budget is hit before `maxItems` is filled, a         non-empty cursor is returned at the current position.         **Scan-budget scope.** The per-call budget bounds *this view's* phase-1 loop (the         number of candidates inspected here), NOT the work inside a single underlying         `indexer.getAnchorsBySchemaAndAddressList` call. That indexer call can itself scan up         to `total` raw positions internally to fill one page when the array is dense with         revoked / non-lens entries — shared behavior with the sibling         `getDirectoryPageBySchemaAndAddressList`, which relies on the same indexer call.

getFilesAtPath(bytes32,address[],bytes32.notice: PIN-based file listing with cross-attester dedup. Opaque-cursor paginated         (ADR-0036, ADR-0041). For each attester, reads the active PIN target         (cardinality 1) at `_activeBySlot[anchorUID][attester][schema]` — Shape A         file-placement reads.         Earlier attesters win on duplicate targets (ADR-0031 first-attester-wins).         Filtered-out entries (targets claimed by an earlier attester) still advance         the walker; `maxItems` bounds result size, not work.
